variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CASSANDRA_CONSISTENCY_LEVEL
documentation: The consistency level of the query. Based on consistency values from [CQL](https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/dml/dmlConfigConsistency.html).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CASSANDRA_COORDINATOR_DC
documentation: The data center of the coordinating node for a query.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CASSANDRA_COORDINATOR_ID
documentation: The ID of the coordinating node for a query.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CASSANDRA_IDEMPOTENCE
documentation: Whether or not the query is idempotent.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CASSANDRA_PAGE_SIZE
documentation: The fetch size used for paging, i.e. how many rows will be returned at once.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CASSANDRA_SPECULATIVE_EXECUTION_COUNT
documentation: The number of times a query was speculatively executed. Not set or `0` if the query was not executed speculatively.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CASSANDRA_TABLE
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `db.collection.name`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CLIENT_CONNECTION_POOL_NAME
documentation: The name of the connection pool; unique within the instrumented application. In case the connection pool implementation doesn't provide a name, instrumentation SHOULD use a combination of parameters that would make the name unique, for example, combining attributes `server.address`, `server.port`, and `db.namespace`, formatted as `server.address:server.port/db.namespace`. Instrumentations that generate connection pool name following different patterns SHOULD document it.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CLIENT_CONNECTION_STATE
documentation: The state of a connection in the pool.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CLIENT_CONNECTIONS_POOL_NAME
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `db.client.connection.pool.name`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CLIENT_CONNECTIONS_STATE
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `db.client.connection.state`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COLLECTION_NAME
documentation:
The name of a collection (table, container) within the database.
Note: It is RECOMMENDED to capture the value as provided by the application without attempting to do any case normalization.
If the collection name is parsed from the query text, it SHOULD be the first collection name found in the query and it SHOULD match the value provided in the query text including any schema and database name prefix.
For batch operations, if the individual operations are known to have the same collection name then that collection name SHOULD be used, otherwise `db.collection.name` SHOULD NOT be captured.
This attribute has stability level RELEASE CANDIDATE.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CONNECTION_STRING
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `server.address` and `server.port`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COSMOSDB_CLIENT_ID
documentation: Unique Cosmos client instance id.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COSMOSDB_CONNECTION_MODE
documentation: Cosmos client connection mode.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COSMOSDB_CONTAINER
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `db.collection.name`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COSMOSDB_OPERATION_TYPE
documentation: Cosmos DB Operation Type.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COSMOSDB_REQUEST_CHARGE
documentation: RU consumed for that operation.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COSMOSDB_REQUEST_CONTENT_LENGTH
documentation: Request payload size in bytes.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COSMOSDB_STATUS_CODE
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `db.response.status_code`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COSMOSDB_SUB_STATUS_CODE
documentation: Cosmos DB sub status code.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_ELASTICSEARCH_CLUSTER_NAME
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `db.namespace`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_ELASTICSEARCH_NODE_NAME
documentation: Represents the human-readable identifier of the node/instance to which a request was routed.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_ELASTICSEARCH_PATH_PARTS_TEMPLATE
documentation:
A dynamic value in the url path.
Note: Many Elasticsearch url paths allow dynamic values. These SHOULD be recorded in span attributes in the format `db.elasticsearch.path_parts.<key>`, where `<key>` is the url path part name. The implementation SHOULD reference the [elasticsearch schema](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/elastic/elasticsearch-specification/main/output/schema/schema.json) in order to map the path part values to their names.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_INSTANCE_ID
documentation: Deprecated: Deprecated, no general replacement at this time. For Elasticsearch, use `db.elasticsearch.node.name` instead.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_JDBC_DRIVER_CLASSNAME
documentation: Deprecated: Removed as not used.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_MONGODB_COLLECTION
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `db.collection.name`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_MSSQL_INSTANCE_NAME
documentation: Deprecated: Deprecated, no replacement at this time.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_NAME
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `db.namespace`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_NAMESPACE
documentation:
The name of the database, fully qualified within the server address and port.
Note: If a database system has multiple namespace components, they SHOULD be concatenated (potentially using database system specific conventions) from most general to most specific namespace component, and more specific namespaces SHOULD NOT be captured without the more general namespaces, to ensure that "startswith" queries for the more general namespaces will be valid.
Semantic conventions for individual database systems SHOULD document what `db.namespace` means in the context of that system.
It is RECOMMENDED to capture the value as provided by the application without attempting to do any case normalization.
This attribute has stability level RELEASE CANDIDATE.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_OPERATION
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `db.operation.name`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_OPERATION_BATCH_SIZE
documentation:
The number of queries included in a batch operation.
Note: Operations are only considered batches when they contain two or more operations, and so `db.operation.batch.size` SHOULD never be `1`.
This attribute has stability level RELEASE CANDIDATE.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_OPERATION_NAME
documentation:
The name of the operation or command being executed.
Note: It is RECOMMENDED to capture the value as provided by the application without attempting to do any case normalization.
If the operation name is parsed from the query text, it SHOULD be the first operation name found in the query.
For batch operations, if the individual operations are known to have the same operation name then that operation name SHOULD be used prepended by `BATCH `, otherwise `db.operation.name` SHOULD be `BATCH` or some other database system specific term if more applicable.
This attribute has stability level RELEASE CANDIDATE.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_QUERY_PARAMETER_TEMPLATE
documentation:
A query parameter used in `db.query.text`, with `<key>` being the parameter name, and the attribute value being a string representation of the parameter value.
Note: Query parameters should only be captured when `db.query.text` is parameterized with placeholders.
If a parameter has no name and instead is referenced only by index, then `<key>` SHOULD be the 0-based index.
This attribute has stability level RELEASE CANDIDATE.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_QUERY_TEXT
documentation:
The database query being executed.
Note: For sanitization see [Sanitization of `db.query.text`](../../docs/database/database-spans.md#sanitization-of-dbquerytext).
For batch operations, if the individual operations are known to have the same query text then that query text SHOULD be used, otherwise all of the individual query texts SHOULD be concatenated with separator `; ` or some other database system specific separator if more applicable.
Even though parameterized query text can potentially have sensitive data, by using a parameterized query the user is giving a strong signal that any sensitive data will be passed as parameter values, and the benefit to observability of capturing the static part of the query text by default outweighs the risk.
This attribute has stability level RELEASE CANDIDATE.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_REDIS_DATABASE_INDEX
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `db.namespace`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_RESPONSE_STATUS_CODE
documentation:
Database response status code.
Note: The status code returned by the database. Usually it represents an error code, but may also represent partial success, warning, or differentiate between various types of successful outcomes.
Semantic conventions for individual database systems SHOULD document what `db.response.status_code` means in the context of that system.
This attribute has stability level RELEASE CANDIDATE.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_SQL_TABLE
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `db.collection.name`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_STATEMENT
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `db.query.text`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_SYSTEM
documentation:
The database management system (DBMS) product as identified by the client instrumentation.
Note: The actual DBMS may differ from the one identified by the client. For example, when using PostgreSQL client libraries to connect to a CockroachDB, the `db.system` is set to `postgresql` based on the instrumentation's best knowledge.
This attribute has stability level RELEASE CANDIDATE.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_USER
documentation: Deprecated: No replacement at this time.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.source_attributes.SOURCE_ADDRESS
documentation:
Source address - domain name if available without reverse DNS lookup; otherwise, IP address or Unix domain socket name.
Note: When observed from the destination side, and when communicating through an intermediary, `source.address` SHOULD represent the source address behind any intermediaries, for example proxies, if it's available.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.source_attributes.SOURCE_PORT
documentation: Source port number.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_BATCH_MESSAGE_COUNT
documentation:
The number of messages sent, received, or processed in the scope of the batching operation.
Note: Instrumentations SHOULD NOT set `messaging.batch.message_count` on spans that operate with a single message. When a messaging client library supports both batch and single-message API for the same operation, instrumentations SHOULD use `messaging.batch.message_count` for batching APIs and SHOULD NOT use it for single-message APIs.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_CLIENT_ID
documentation: A unique identifier for the client that consumes or produces a message.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_CONSUMER_GROUP_NAME
documentation:
The name of the consumer group with which a consumer is associated.
Note: Semantic conventions for individual messaging systems SHOULD document whether `messaging.consumer.group.name` is applicable and what it means in the context of that system.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_ANONYMOUS
documentation: A boolean that is true if the message destination is anonymous (could be unnamed or have auto-generated name).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_NAME
documentation:
The message destination name.
Note: Destination name SHOULD uniquely identify a specific queue, topic or other entity within the broker. If
the broker doesn't have such notion, the destination name SHOULD uniquely identify the broker.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_PARTITION_ID
documentation: The identifier of the partition messages are sent to or received from, unique within the `messaging.destination.name`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_SUBSCRIPTION_NAME
documentation:
The name of the destination subscription from which a message is consumed.
Note: Semantic conventions for individual messaging systems SHOULD document whether `messaging.destination.subscription.name` is applicable and what it means in the context of that system.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_TEMPLATE
documentation:
Low cardinality representation of the messaging destination name.
Note: Destination names could be constructed from templates. An example would be a destination name involving a user name or product id. Although the destination name in this case is of high cardinality, the underlying template is of low cardinality and can be effectively used for grouping and aggregation.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_TEMPORARY
documentation: A boolean that is true if the message destination is temporary and might not exist anymore after messages are processed.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_PUBLISH_ANONYMOUS
documentation: Deprecated: No replacement at this time.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_PUBLISH_NAME
documentation: Deprecated: No replacement at this time.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_EVENTHUBS_CONSUMER_GROUP
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `messaging.consumer.group.name`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_EVENTHUBS_MESSAGE_ENQUEUED_TIME
documentation: The UTC epoch seconds at which the message has been accepted and stored in the entity.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_GCP_PUBSUB_MESSAGE_ACK_DEADLINE
documentation: The ack deadline in seconds set for the modify ack deadline request.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_GCP_PUBSUB_MESSAGE_ACK_ID
documentation: The ack id for a given message.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_GCP_PUBSUB_MESSAGE_DELIVERY_ATTEMPT
documentation: The delivery attempt for a given message.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_GCP_PUBSUB_MESSAGE_ORDERING_KEY
documentation: The ordering key for a given message. If the attribute is not present, the message does not have an ordering key.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_CONSUMER_GROUP
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `messaging.consumer.group.name`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_DESTINATION_PARTITION
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `messaging.destination.partition.id`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_MESSAGE_KEY
documentation:
Message keys in Kafka are used for grouping alike messages to ensure they're processed on the same partition. They differ from `messaging.message.id` in that they're not unique. If the key is `null`, the attribute MUST NOT be set.
Note: If the key type is not string, it's string representation has to be supplied for the attribute. If the key has no unambiguous, canonical string form, don't include its value.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_MESSAGE_OFFSET
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `messaging.kafka.offset`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_MESSAGE_TOMBSTONE
documentation: A boolean that is true if the message is a tombstone.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_OFFSET
documentation: The offset of a record in the corresponding Kafka partition.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_MESSAGE_BODY_SIZE
documentation:
The size of the message body in bytes.
Note: This can refer to both the compressed or uncompressed body size. If both sizes are known, the uncompressed
body size should be used.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_MESSAGE_CONVERSATION_ID
documentation: The conversation ID identifying the conversation to which the message belongs, represented as a string. Sometimes called "Correlation ID".

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_MESSAGE_ENVELOPE_SIZE
documentation:
The size of the message body and metadata in bytes.
Note: This can refer to both the compressed or uncompressed size. If both sizes are known, the uncompressed
size should be used.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_MESSAGE_ID
documentation: A value used by the messaging system as an identifier for the message, represented as a string.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_OPERATION
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `messaging.operation.type`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_OPERATION_NAME
documentation: The system-specific name of the messaging operation.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_OPERATION_TYPE
documentation:
A string identifying the type of the messaging operation.
Note: If a custom value is used, it MUST be of low cardinality.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_RABBITMQ_DESTINATION_ROUTING_KEY
documentation: RabbitMQ message routing key.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_RABBITMQ_MESSAGE_DELIVERY_TAG
documentation: RabbitMQ message delivery tag.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_CLIENT_GROUP
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `messaging.consumer.group.name` on the consumer spans. No replacement for producer spans.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_CONSUMPTION_MODEL
documentation: Model of message consumption. This only applies to consumer spans.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_DELAY_TIME_LEVEL
documentation: The delay time level for delay message, which determines the message delay time.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_DELIVERY_TIMESTAMP
documentation: The timestamp in milliseconds that the delay message is expected to be delivered to consumer.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_GROUP
documentation: It is essential for FIFO message. Messages that belong to the same message group are always processed one by one within the same consumer group.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_KEYS
documentation: Key(s) of message, another way to mark message besides message id.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_TAG
documentation: The secondary classifier of message besides topic.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_TYPE
documentation: Type of message.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_NAMESPACE
documentation: Namespace of RocketMQ resources, resources in different namespaces are individual.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_SERVICEBUS_DESTINATION_SUBSCRIPTION_NAME
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `messaging.destination.subscription.name`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_SERVICEBUS_DISPOSITION_STATUS
documentation: Describes the [settlement type](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/service-bus-messaging/message-transfers-locks-settlement#peeklock).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_SERVICEBUS_MESSAGE_DELIVERY_COUNT
documentation: Number of deliveries that have been attempted for this message.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_SERVICEBUS_MESSAGE_ENQUEUED_TIME
documentation: The UTC epoch seconds at which the message has been accepted and stored in the entity.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_SYSTEM
documentation:
The messaging system as identified by the client instrumentation.
Note: The actual messaging system may differ from the one known by the client. For example, when using Kafka client libraries to communicate with Azure Event Hubs, the `messaging.system` is set to `kafka` based on the instrumentation's best knowledge.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MessagingOperationTypeValues.CREATE
documentation: A message is created. "Create" spans always refer to a single message and are used to provide a unique creation context for messages in batch sending scenarios.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MessagingOperationTypeValues.SEND
documentation: One or more messages are provided for sending to an intermediary. If a single message is sent, the context of the "Send" span can be used as the creation context and no "Create" span needs to be created.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MessagingOperationTypeValues.RECEIVE
documentation: One or more messages are requested by a consumer. This operation refers to pull-based scenarios, where consumers explicitly call methods of messaging SDKs to receive messages.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MessagingOperationTypeValues.PROCESS
documentation: One or more messages are processed by a consumer.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MessagingOperationTypeValues.SETTLE
documentation: One or more messages are settled.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MessagingOperationTypeValues.DELIVER
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `process`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MessagingOperationTypeValues.PUBLISH
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `send`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MessagingRocketmqConsumptionModelValues.CLUSTERING
documentation: Clustering consumption model.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MessagingRocketmqConsumptionModelValues.BROADCASTING
documentation: Broadcasting consumption model.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MessagingRocketmqMessageTypeValues.NORMAL
documentation: Normal message.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MessagingRocketmqMessageTypeValues.FIFO
documentation: FIFO message.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MessagingRocketmqMessageTypeValues.DELAY
documentation: Delay message.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.vcs_attributes.VCS_REPOSITORY_CHANGE_ID
documentation: The ID of the change (pull request/merge request) if applicable. This is usually a unique (within repository) identifier generated by the VCS system.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.vcs_attributes.VCS_REPOSITORY_CHANGE_TITLE
documentation: The human readable title of the change (pull request/merge request). This title is often a brief summary of the change and may get merged in to a ref as the commit summary.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.vcs_attributes.VCS_REPOSITORY_REF_NAME
documentation: The name of the [reference](https://git-scm.com/docs/gitglossary#def_ref) such as **branch** or **tag** in the repository.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.vcs_attributes.VCS_REPOSITORY_REF_REVISION
documentation:
The revision, literally [revised version](https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/revision), The revision most often refers to a commit object in Git, or a revision number in SVN.
Note: The revision can be a full [hash value (see glossary)](https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/FIPS/NIST.FIPS.186-5.pdf),
of the recorded change to a ref within a repository pointing to a
commit [commit](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-commit) object. It does
not necessarily have to be a hash; it can simply define a
[revision number](https://svnbook.red-bean.com/en/1.7/svn.tour.revs.specifiers.html)
which is an integer that is monotonically increasing. In cases where
it is identical to the `ref.name`, it SHOULD still be included. It is
up to the implementer to decide which value to set as the revision
based on the VCS system and situational context.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.vcs_attributes.VCS_REPOSITORY_REF_TYPE
documentation: The type of the [reference](https://git-scm.com/docs/gitglossary#def_ref) in the repository.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.vcs_attributes.VCS_REPOSITORY_URL_FULL
documentation: The [URL](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/URL) of the repository providing the complete address in order to locate and identify the repository.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.vcs_attributes.VcsRepositoryRefTypeValues.BRANCH
documentation: [branch](https://git-scm.com/docs/gitglossary#Documentation/gitglossary.txt-aiddefbranchabranch).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.vcs_attributes.VcsRepositoryRefTypeValues.TAG
documentation: [tag](https://git-scm.com/docs/gitglossary#Documentation/gitglossary.txt-aiddeftagatag).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.user_agent_attributes.USER_AGENT_NAME
documentation:
Name of the user-agent extracted from original. Usually refers to the browser's name.
Note: [Example](https://www.whatsmyua.info) of extracting browser's name from original string. In the case of using a user-agent for non-browser products, such as microservices with multiple names/versions inside the `user_agent.original`, the most significant name SHOULD be selected. In such a scenario it should align with `user_agent.version`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.user_agent_attributes.USER_AGENT_ORIGINAL
documentation: Deprecated in favor of stable {py:const}`opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.user_agent_attributes.USER_AGENT_ORIGINAL`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.user_agent_attributes.USER_AGENT_VERSION
documentation:
Version of the user-agent extracted from original. Usually refers to the browser's version.
Note: [Example](https://www.whatsmyua.info) of extracting browser's version from original string. In the case of using a user-agent for non-browser products, such as microservices with multiple names/versions inside the `user_agent.original`, the most significant version SHOULD be selected. In such a scenario it should align with `user_agent.name`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.service_attributes.SERVICE_INSTANCE_ID
documentation:
The string ID of the service instance.
Note: MUST be unique for each instance of the same `service.namespace,service.name` pair (in other words
`service.namespace,service.name,service.instance.id` triplet MUST be globally unique). The ID helps to
distinguish instances of the same service that exist at the same time (e.g. instances of a horizontally scaled
service).

Implementations, such as SDKs, are recommended to generate a random Version 1 or Version 4 [RFC
4122](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc4122.txt) UUID, but are free to use an inherent unique ID as the source of
this value if stability is desirable. In that case, the ID SHOULD be used as source of a UUID Version 5 and
SHOULD use the following UUID as the namespace: `4d63009a-8d0f-11ee-aad7-4c796ed8e320`.

UUIDs are typically recommended, as only an opaque value for the purposes of identifying a service instance is
needed. Similar to what can be seen in the man page for the
[`/etc/machine-id`](https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/machine-id.html) file, the underlying
data, such as pod name and namespace should be treated as confidential, being the user's choice to expose it
or not via another resource attribute.

For applications running behind an application server (like unicorn), we do not recommend using one identifier
for all processes participating in the application. Instead, it's recommended each division (e.g. a worker
thread in unicorn) to have its own instance.id.

It's not recommended for a Collector to set `service.instance.id` if it can't unambiguously determine the
service instance that is generating that telemetry. For instance, creating an UUID based on `pod.name` will
likely be wrong, as the Collector might not know from which container within that pod the telemetry originated.
However, Collectors can set the `service.instance.id` if they can unambiguously determine the service instance
for that telemetry. This is typically the case for scraping receivers, as they know the target address and
port.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.service_attributes.SERVICE_NAME
documentation: Deprecated in favor of stable {py:const}`opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.service_attributes.SERVICE_NAME`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.service_attributes.SERVICE_NAMESPACE
documentation:
A namespace for `service.name`.
Note: A string value having a meaning that helps to distinguish a group of services, for example the team name that owns a group of services. `service.name` is expected to be unique within the same namespace. If `service.namespace` is not specified in the Resource then `service.name` is expected to be unique for all services that have no explicit namespace defined (so the empty/unspecified namespace is simply one more valid namespace). Zero-length namespace string is assumed equal to unspecified namespace.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.service_attributes.SERVICE_VERSION
documentation: Deprecated in favor of stable {py:const}`opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.service_attributes.SERVICE_VERSION`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CIPHER
documentation:
String indicating the [cipher](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5246#appendix-A.5) used during the current connection.
Note: The values allowed for `tls.cipher` MUST be one of the `Descriptions` of the [registered TLS Cipher Suits](https://www.iana.org/assignments/tls-parameters/tls-parameters.xhtml#table-tls-parameters-4).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_CERTIFICATE
documentation: PEM-encoded stand-alone certificate offered by the client. This is usually mutually-exclusive of `client.certificate_chain` since this value also exists in that list.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_CERTIFICATE_CHAIN
documentation: Array of PEM-encoded certificates that make up the certificate chain offered by the client. This is usually mutually-exclusive of `client.certificate` since that value should be the first certificate in the chain.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_HASH_MD5
documentation: Certificate fingerprint using the MD5 digest of DER-encoded version of certificate offered by the client. For consistency with other hash values, this value should be formatted as an uppercase hash.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_HASH_SHA1
documentation: Certificate fingerprint using the SHA1 digest of DER-encoded version of certificate offered by the client. For consistency with other hash values, this value should be formatted as an uppercase hash.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_HASH_SHA256
documentation: Certificate fingerprint using the SHA256 digest of DER-encoded version of certificate offered by the client. For consistency with other hash values, this value should be formatted as an uppercase hash.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_ISSUER
documentation: Distinguished name of [subject](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5280#section-4.1.2.6) of the issuer of the x.509 certificate presented by the client.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_JA3
documentation: A hash that identifies clients based on how they perform an SSL/TLS handshake.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_NOT_AFTER
documentation: Date/Time indicating when client certificate is no longer considered valid.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_NOT_BEFORE
documentation: Date/Time indicating when client certificate is first considered valid.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_SERVER_NAME
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `server.address`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_SUBJECT
documentation: Distinguished name of subject of the x.509 certificate presented by the client.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_SUPPORTED_CIPHERS
documentation: Array of ciphers offered by the client during the client hello.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CURVE
documentation: String indicating the curve used for the given cipher, when applicable.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_ESTABLISHED
documentation: Boolean flag indicating if the TLS negotiation was successful and transitioned to an encrypted tunnel.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_NEXT_PROTOCOL
documentation: String indicating the protocol being tunneled. Per the values in the [IANA registry](https://www.iana.org/assignments/tls-extensiontype-values/tls-extensiontype-values.xhtml#alpn-protocol-ids), this string should be lower case.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_PROTOCOL_NAME
documentation: Normalized lowercase protocol name parsed from original string of the negotiated [SSL/TLS protocol version](https://www.openssl.org/docs/man1.1.1/man3/SSL_get_version.html#RETURN-VALUES).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_PROTOCOL_VERSION
documentation: Numeric part of the version parsed from the original string of the negotiated [SSL/TLS protocol version](https://www.openssl.org/docs/man1.1.1/man3/SSL_get_version.html#RETURN-VALUES).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_RESUMED
documentation: Boolean flag indicating if this TLS connection was resumed from an existing TLS negotiation.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_CERTIFICATE
documentation: PEM-encoded stand-alone certificate offered by the server. This is usually mutually-exclusive of `server.certificate_chain` since this value also exists in that list.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_CERTIFICATE_CHAIN
documentation: Array of PEM-encoded certificates that make up the certificate chain offered by the server. This is usually mutually-exclusive of `server.certificate` since that value should be the first certificate in the chain.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_HASH_MD5
documentation: Certificate fingerprint using the MD5 digest of DER-encoded version of certificate offered by the server. For consistency with other hash values, this value should be formatted as an uppercase hash.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_HASH_SHA1
documentation: Certificate fingerprint using the SHA1 digest of DER-encoded version of certificate offered by the server. For consistency with other hash values, this value should be formatted as an uppercase hash.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_HASH_SHA256
documentation: Certificate fingerprint using the SHA256 digest of DER-encoded version of certificate offered by the server. For consistency with other hash values, this value should be formatted as an uppercase hash.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_ISSUER
documentation: Distinguished name of [subject](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5280#section-4.1.2.6) of the issuer of the x.509 certificate presented by the client.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_JA3S
documentation: A hash that identifies servers based on how they perform an SSL/TLS handshake.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_NOT_AFTER
documentation: Date/Time indicating when server certificate is no longer considered valid.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_NOT_BEFORE
documentation: Date/Time indicating when server certificate is first considered valid.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_SUBJECT
documentation: Distinguished name of subject of the x.509 certificate presented by the server.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TlsProtocolNameValues.SSL
documentation: ssl.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TlsProtocolNameValues.TLS
documentation: tls.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_ARGS_COUNT
documentation:
Length of the process.command_args array.
Note: This field can be useful for querying or performing bucket analysis on how many arguments were provided to start a process. More arguments may be an indication of suspicious activity.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_COMMAND
documentation: The command used to launch the process (i.e. the command name). On Linux based systems, can be set to the zeroth string in `proc/[pid]/cmdline`. On Windows, can be set to the first parameter extracted from `GetCommandLineW`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_COMMAND_ARGS
documentation: All the command arguments (including the command/executable itself) as received by the process. On Linux-based systems (and some other Unixoid systems supporting procfs), can be set according to the list of null-delimited strings extracted from `proc/[pid]/cmdline`. For libc-based executables, this would be the full argv vector passed to `main`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_COMMAND_LINE
documentation: The full command used to launch the process as a single string representing the full command. On Windows, can be set to the result of `GetCommandLineW`. Do not set this if you have to assemble it just for monitoring; use `process.command_args` instead.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_CONTEXT_SWITCH_TYPE
documentation: Specifies whether the context switches for this data point were voluntary or involuntary.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_CPU_STATE
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `cpu.mode`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_CREATION_TIME
documentation: The date and time the process was created, in ISO 8601 format.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_EXECUTABLE_BUILD_ID_GNU
documentation: The GNU build ID as found in the `.note.gnu.build-id` ELF section (hex string).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_EXECUTABLE_BUILD_ID_GO
documentation: The Go build ID as retrieved by `go tool buildid <go executable>`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_EXECUTABLE_BUILD_ID_PROFILING
documentation: Profiling specific build ID for executables. See the OTel specification for Profiles for more information.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_EXECUTABLE_NAME
documentation: The name of the process executable. On Linux based systems, can be set to the `Name` in `proc/[pid]/status`. On Windows, can be set to the base name of `GetProcessImageFileNameW`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_EXECUTABLE_PATH
documentation: The full path to the process executable. On Linux based systems, can be set to the target of `proc/[pid]/exe`. On Windows, can be set to the result of `GetProcessImageFileNameW`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_EXIT_CODE
documentation: The exit code of the process.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_EXIT_TIME
documentation: The date and time the process exited, in ISO 8601 format.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_GROUP_LEADER_PID
documentation: The PID of the process's group leader. This is also the process group ID (PGID) of the process.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_INTERACTIVE
documentation: Whether the process is connected to an interactive shell.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_OWNER
documentation: The username of the user that owns the process.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_PAGING_FAULT_TYPE
documentation: The type of page fault for this data point. Type `major` is for major/hard page faults, and `minor` is for minor/soft page faults.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_PARENT_PID
documentation: Parent Process identifier (PPID).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_PID
documentation: Process identifier (PID).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_REAL_USER_ID
documentation: The real user ID (RUID) of the process.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_REAL_USER_NAME
documentation: The username of the real user of the process.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_RUNTIME_DESCRIPTION
documentation: An additional description about the runtime of the process, for example a specific vendor customization of the runtime environment.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_RUNTIME_NAME
documentation: The name of the runtime of this process.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_RUNTIME_VERSION
documentation: The version of the runtime of this process, as returned by the runtime without modification.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_SAVED_USER_ID
documentation: The saved user ID (SUID) of the process.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_SAVED_USER_NAME
documentation: The username of the saved user.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_SESSION_LEADER_PID
documentation: The PID of the process's session leader. This is also the session ID (SID) of the process.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_TITLE
documentation:
Process title (proctitle).
Note: In many Unix-like systems, process title (proctitle), is the string that represents the name or command line of a running process, displayed by system monitoring tools like ps, top, and htop.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_USER_ID
documentation: The effective user ID (EUID) of the process.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_USER_NAME
documentation: The username of the effective user of the process.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_VPID
documentation:
Virtual process identifier.
Note: The process ID within a PID namespace. This is not necessarily unique across all processes on the host but it is unique within the process namespace that the process exists within.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_WORKING_DIRECTORY
documentation: The working directory of the process.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.ProcessContextSwitchTypeValues.VOLUNTARY
documentation: voluntary.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.ProcessContextSwitchTypeValues.INVOLUNTARY
documentation: involuntary.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.ProcessCpuStateValues.SYSTEM
documentation: system.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.ProcessCpuStateValues.USER
documentation: user.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.ProcessCpuStateValues.WAIT
documentation: wait.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.ProcessPagingFaultTypeValues.MAJOR
documentation: major.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.ProcessPagingFaultTypeValues.MINOR
documentation: minor.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_CONNECT_RPC_ERROR_CODE
documentation: The [error codes](https://connect.build/docs/protocol/#error-codes) of the Connect request. Error codes are always string values.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_CONNECT_RPC_REQUEST_METADATA_TEMPLATE
documentation:
Connect request metadata, `<key>` being the normalized Connect Metadata key (lowercase), the value being the metadata values.
Note: Instrumentations SHOULD require an explicit configuration of which metadata values are to be captured. Including all request metadata values can be a security risk - explicit configuration helps avoid leaking sensitive information.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_CONNECT_RPC_RESPONSE_METADATA_TEMPLATE
documentation:
Connect response metadata, `<key>` being the normalized Connect Metadata key (lowercase), the value being the metadata values.
Note: Instrumentations SHOULD require an explicit configuration of which metadata values are to be captured. Including all response metadata values can be a security risk - explicit configuration helps avoid leaking sensitive information.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_GRPC_REQUEST_METADATA_TEMPLATE
documentation:
gRPC request metadata, `<key>` being the normalized gRPC Metadata key (lowercase), the value being the metadata values.
Note: Instrumentations SHOULD require an explicit configuration of which metadata values are to be captured. Including all request metadata values can be a security risk - explicit configuration helps avoid leaking sensitive information.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_GRPC_RESPONSE_METADATA_TEMPLATE
documentation:
gRPC response metadata, `<key>` being the normalized gRPC Metadata key (lowercase), the value being the metadata values.
Note: Instrumentations SHOULD require an explicit configuration of which metadata values are to be captured. Including all response metadata values can be a security risk - explicit configuration helps avoid leaking sensitive information.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_GRPC_STATUS_CODE
documentation: The [numeric status code](https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/v1.33.2/doc/statuscodes.md) of the gRPC request.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_JSONRPC_ERROR_CODE
documentation: `error.code` property of response if it is an error response.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_JSONRPC_ERROR_MESSAGE
documentation: `error.message` property of response if it is an error response.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_JSONRPC_REQUEST_ID
documentation: `id` property of request or response. Since protocol allows id to be int, string, `null` or missing (for notifications), value is expected to be cast to string for simplicity. Use empty string in case of `null` value. Omit entirely if this is a notification.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_JSONRPC_VERSION
documentation: Protocol version as in `jsonrpc` property of request/response. Since JSON-RPC 1.0 doesn't specify this, the value can be omitted.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_MESSAGE_COMPRESSED_SIZE
documentation: Compressed size of the message in bytes.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_MESSAGE_ID
documentation:
MUST be calculated as two different counters starting from `1` one for sent messages and one for received message.
Note: This way we guarantee that the values will be consistent between different implementations.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_MESSAGE_TYPE
documentation: Whether this is a received or sent message.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_MESSAGE_UNCOMPRESSED_SIZE
documentation: Uncompressed size of the message in bytes.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_METHOD
documentation:
The name of the (logical) method being called, must be equal to the $method part in the span name.
Note: This is the logical name of the method from the RPC interface perspective, which can be different from the name of any implementing method/function. The `code.function` attribute may be used to store the latter (e.g., method actually executing the call on the server side, RPC client stub method on the client side).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_SERVICE
documentation:
The full (logical) name of the service being called, including its package name, if applicable.
Note: This is the logical name of the service from the RPC interface perspective, which can be different from the name of any implementing class. The `code.namespace` attribute may be used to store the latter (despite the attribute name, it may include a class name; e.g., class with method actually executing the call on the server side, RPC client stub class on the client side).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_SYSTEM
documentation: A string identifying the remoting system. See below for a list of well-known identifiers.

documentation: Total CPU time consumed

documentation: Container's CPU usage, measured in cpus. Range from 0 to the number of allocatable CPUs

functions.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.container_metrics.create_container_disk_io
documentation: Disk bytes for the container

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.container_metrics.CONTAINER_CPU_TIME
documentation:
Total CPU time consumed
Instrument: counter
Unit: s
Note: Total CPU time consumed by the specific container on all available CPU cores.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.container_metrics.CONTAINER_CPU_USAGE
documentation:
Container's CPU usage, measured in cpus. Range from 0 to the number of allocatable CPUs
Instrument: gauge
Unit: {cpu}
Note: CPU usage of the specific container on all available CPU cores, averaged over the sample window.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.container_metrics.CONTAINER_DISK_IO
documentation:
Disk bytes for the container
Instrument: counter
Unit: By
Note: The total number of bytes read/written successfully (aggregated from all disks).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.container_metrics.CONTAINER_MEMORY_USAGE
documentation:
Memory usage of the container
Instrument: counter
Unit: By
Note: Memory usage of the container.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.container_metrics.CONTAINER_NETWORK_IO
documentation:
Network bytes for the container
Instrument: counter
Unit: By
Note: The number of bytes sent/received on all network interfaces by the container.

functions.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.hw_metrics.create_hw_energy
documentation: Energy consumed by the component

documentation: Number of errors encountered by the component

functions.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.hw_metrics.create_hw_power
documentation: Instantaneous power consumed by the component

functions.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.hw_metrics.create_hw_status
documentation: Operational status: `1` (true) or `0` (false) for each of the possible states

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.hw_metrics.HW_ENERGY
documentation:
Energy consumed by the component
Instrument: counter
Unit: J

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.hw_metrics.HW_ERRORS
opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.hw_metrics.HW_POWER
documentation:
Instantaneous power consumed by the component
Instrument: gauge
Unit: W
Note: It is recommended to report `hw.energy` instead of `hw.power` when possible.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.hw_metrics.HW_STATUS
documentation:
Operational status: `1` (true) or `0` (false) for each of the possible states
Instrument: updowncounter
Unit: 1
Note: `hw.status` is currently specified as an *UpDownCounter* but would ideally be represented using a [*StateSet* as defined in OpenMetrics](https://github.com/OpenObservability/OpenMetrics/blob/main/specification/OpenMetrics.md#stateset). This semantic convention will be updated once *StateSet* is specified in OpenTelemetry. This planned change is not expected to have any consequence on the way users query their timeseries backend to retrieve the values of `hw.status` over time.
