class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.SCHEMA_URL
documentation: The URL of the OpenTelemetry schema for these keys and values.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CLIENT_ADDRESS
documentation:
Client address - unix domain socket name, IPv4 or IPv6 address.
Note: When observed from the server side, and when communicating through an intermediary, `client.address` SHOULD represent client address behind any intermediaries (e.g. proxies) if it's available.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CLIENT_PORT
documentation:
Client port number.
Note: When observed from the server side, and when communicating through an intermediary, `client.port` SHOULD represent client port behind any intermediaries (e.g. proxies) if it's available.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CLIENT_SOCKET_ADDRESS
documentation: Immediate client peer address - unix domain socket name, IPv4 or IPv6 address.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CLIENT_SOCKET_PORT
documentation: Immediate client peer port number.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_METHOD
documentation: Deprecated, use `http.request.method` instead.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_STATUS_CODE
documentation: Deprecated, use `http.response.status_code` instead.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_SCHEME
documentation: Deprecated, use `url.scheme` instead.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_URL
documentation: Deprecated, use `url.full` instead.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_TARGET
documentation: Deprecated, use `url.path` and `url.query` instead.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_REQUEST_CONTENT_LENGTH
documentation: Deprecated, use `http.request.body.size` instead.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_RESPONSE_CONTENT_LENGTH
documentation: Deprecated, use `http.response.body.size` instead.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_SOCK_PEER_NAME
documentation: Deprecated, use `server.socket.domain` on client spans.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_SOCK_PEER_ADDR
documentation: Deprecated, use `server.socket.address` on client spans and `client.socket.address` on server spans.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_SOCK_PEER_PORT
documentation: Deprecated, use `server.socket.port` on client spans and `client.socket.port` on server spans.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_PEER_NAME
documentation: Deprecated, use `server.address` on client spans and `client.address` on server spans.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_PEER_PORT
documentation: Deprecated, use `server.port` on client spans and `client.port` on server spans.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_HOST_NAME
documentation: Deprecated, use `server.address`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_HOST_PORT
documentation: Deprecated, use `server.port`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_SOCK_HOST_ADDR
documentation: Deprecated, use `server.socket.address`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_SOCK_HOST_PORT
documentation: Deprecated, use `server.socket.port`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_TRANSPORT
documentation: Deprecated, use `network.transport`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_PROTOCOL_NAME
documentation: Deprecated, use `network.protocol.name`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_PROTOCOL_VERSION
documentation: Deprecated, use `network.protocol.version`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_SOCK_FAMILY
documentation: Deprecated, use `network.transport` and `network.type`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DESTINATION_DOMAIN
documentation:
The domain name of the destination system.
Note: This value may be a host name, a fully qualified domain name, or another host naming format.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DESTINATION_ADDRESS
documentation: Peer address, for example IP address or UNIX socket name.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DESTINATION_PORT
documentation: Peer port number.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.EXCEPTION_TYPE
documentation: The type of the exception (its fully-qualified class name, if applicable). The dynamic type of the exception should be preferred over the static type in languages that support it.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.EXCEPTION_MESSAGE
documentation: The exception message.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.EXCEPTION_STACKTRACE
documentation: A stacktrace as a string in the natural representation for the language runtime. The representation is to be determined and documented by each language SIG.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_REQUEST_METHOD
documentation:
HTTP request method.
Note: HTTP request method value SHOULD be "known" to the instrumentation.
By default, this convention defines "known" methods as the ones listed in [RFC9110](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#name-methods)
and the PATCH method defined in [RFC5789](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5789.html).

If the HTTP request method is not known to instrumentation, it MUST set the `http.request.method` attribute to `_OTHER` and, except if reporting a metric, MUST
set the exact method received in the request line as value of the `http.request.method_original` attribute.

If the HTTP instrumentation could end up converting valid HTTP request methods to `_OTHER`, then it MUST provide a way to override
the list of known HTTP methods. If this override is done via environment variable, then the environment variable MUST be named
OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_HTTP_KNOWN_METHODS and support a comma-separated list of case-sensitive known HTTP methods
(this list MUST be a full override of the default known method, it is not a list of known methods in addition to the defaults).

HTTP method names are case-sensitive and `http.request.method` attribute value MUST match a known HTTP method name exactly.
Instrumentations for specific web frameworks that consider HTTP methods to be case insensitive, SHOULD populate a canonical equivalent.
Tracing instrumentations that do so, MUST also set `http.request.method_original` to the original value.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_RESPONSE_STATUS_CODE
documentation: [HTTP response status code](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NETWORK_PROTOCOL_NAME
documentation: [OSI Application Layer](https://osi-model.com/application-layer/) or non-OSI equivalent. The value SHOULD be normalized to lowercase.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NETWORK_PROTOCOL_VERSION
documentation:
Version of the application layer protocol used. See note below.
Note: `network.protocol.version` refers to the version of the protocol used and might be different from the protocol client's version. If the HTTP client used has a version of `0.27.2`, but sends HTTP version `1.1`, this attribute should be set to `1.1`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.SERVER_ADDRESS
documentation:
Host identifier of the ["URI origin"](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#name-uri-origin) HTTP request is sent to.
Note: Determined by using the first of the following that applies

- Host identifier of the [request target](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#target.resource)
  if it's sent in absolute-form
- Host identifier of the `Host` header

SHOULD NOT be set if capturing it would require an extra DNS lookup.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.SERVER_PORT
documentation:
Port identifier of the ["URI origin"](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#name-uri-origin) HTTP request is sent to.
Note: When [request target](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#target.resource) is absolute URI, `server.port` MUST match URI port identifier, otherwise it MUST match `Host` header port identifier.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_ROUTE
documentation:
The matched route (path template in the format used by the respective server framework). See note below.
Note: MUST NOT be populated when this is not supported by the HTTP server framework as the route attribute should have low-cardinality and the URI path can NOT substitute it.
SHOULD include the [application root](/docs/http/http-spans.md#http-server-definitions) if there is one.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.URL_SCHEME
documentation: The [URI scheme](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986#section-3.1) component identifying the used protocol.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.EVENT_NAME
documentation: The name identifies the event.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.EVENT_DOMAIN
documentation:
The domain identifies the business context for the events.
Note: Events across different domains may have same `event.name`, yet be
unrelated events.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.LOG_RECORD_UID
documentation:
A unique identifier for the Log Record.
Note: If an id is provided, other log records with the same id will be considered duplicates and can be removed safely. This means, that two distinguishable log records MUST have different values.
The id MAY be an [Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier (ULID)](https://github.com/ulid/spec), but other identifiers (e.g. UUID) may be used as needed.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FEATURE_FLAG_KEY
documentation: The unique identifier of the feature flag.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FEATURE_FLAG_PROVIDER_NAME
documentation: The name of the service provider that performs the flag evaluation.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FEATURE_FLAG_VARIANT
documentation:
SHOULD be a semantic identifier for a value. If one is unavailable, a stringified version of the value can be used.
Note: A semantic identifier, commonly referred to as a variant, provides a means
for referring to a value without including the value itself. This can
provide additional context for understanding the meaning behind a value.
For example, the variant `red` maybe be used for the value `#c05543`.

A stringified version of the value can be used in situations where a
semantic identifier is unavailable. String representation of the value
should be determined by the implementer.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.LOG_IOSTREAM
documentation: The stream associated with the log. See below for a list of well-known values.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.LOG_FILE_NAME
documentation: The basename of the file.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.LOG_FILE_PATH
documentation: The full path to the file.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.LOG_FILE_NAME_RESOLVED
documentation: The basename of the file, with symlinks resolved.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.LOG_FILE_PATH_RESOLVED
documentation: The full path to the file, with symlinks resolved.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.SERVER_SOCKET_ADDRESS
documentation: Physical server IP address or Unix socket address. If set from the client, should simply use the socket's peer address, and not attempt to find any actual server IP (i.e., if set from client, this may represent some proxy server instead of the logical server).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.POOL
documentation:
Name of the buffer pool.
Note: Pool names are generally obtained via [BufferPoolMXBean#getName()](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.management/java/lang/management/BufferPoolMXBean.html#getName()).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.TYPE
documentation: The type of memory.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.SERVER_SOCKET_DOMAIN
documentation:
The domain name of an immediate peer.
Note: Typically observed from the client side, and represents a proxy or other intermediary domain name.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.SERVER_SOCKET_PORT
documentation: Physical server port.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.SOURCE_DOMAIN
documentation:
The domain name of the source system.
Note: This value may be a host name, a fully qualified domain name, or another host naming format.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.SOURCE_ADDRESS
documentation: Source address, for example IP address or Unix socket name.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.SOURCE_PORT
documentation: Source port number.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_LAMBDA_INVOKED_ARN
documentation:
The full invoked ARN as provided on the `Context` passed to the function (`Lambda-Runtime-Invoked-Function-Arn` header on the `/runtime/invocation/next` applicable).
Note: This may be different from `cloud.resource_id` if an alias is involved.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CLOUDEVENTS_EVENT_ID
documentation: The [event_id](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#id) uniquely identifies the event.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CLOUDEVENTS_EVENT_SOURCE
documentation: The [source](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#source-1) identifies the context in which an event happened.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CLOUDEVENTS_EVENT_SPEC_VERSION
documentation: The [version of the CloudEvents specification](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#specversion) which the event uses.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CLOUDEVENTS_EVENT_TYPE
documentation: The [event_type](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#type) contains a value describing the type of event related to the originating occurrence.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CLOUDEVENTS_EVENT_SUBJECT
documentation: The [subject](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#subject) of the event in the context of the event producer (identified by source).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.OPENTRACING_REF_TYPE
documentation:
Parent-child Reference type.
Note: The causal relationship between a child Span and a parent Span.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_SYSTEM
documentation: An identifier for the database management system (DBMS) product being used. See below for a list of well-known identifiers.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_CONNECTION_STRING
documentation: The connection string used to connect to the database. It is recommended to remove embedded credentials.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_USER
documentation: Username for accessing the database.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_JDBC_DRIVER_CLASSNAME
documentation: The fully-qualified class name of the [Java Database Connectivity (JDBC)](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/technotes/guides/jdbc/) driver used to connect.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_NAME
documentation:
This attribute is used to report the name of the database being accessed. For commands that switch the database, this should be set to the target database (even if the command fails).
Note: In some SQL databases, the database name to be used is called "schema name". In case there are multiple layers that could be considered for database name (e.g. Oracle instance name and schema name), the database name to be used is the more specific layer (e.g. Oracle schema name).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_STATEMENT
documentation: The database statement being executed.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_OPERATION
documentation:
The name of the operation being executed, e.g. the [MongoDB command name](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/#database-operations) such as `findAndModify`, or the SQL keyword.
Note: When setting this to an SQL keyword, it is not recommended to attempt any client-side parsing of `db.statement` just to get this property, but it should be set if the operation name is provided by the library being instrumented. If the SQL statement has an ambiguous operation, or performs more than one operation, this value may be omitted.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NETWORK_TRANSPORT
documentation: [OSI Transport Layer](https://osi-model.com/transport-layer/) or [Inter-process Communication method](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inter-process_communication). The value SHOULD be normalized to lowercase.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NETWORK_TYPE
documentation: [OSI Network Layer](https://osi-model.com/network-layer/) or non-OSI equivalent. The value SHOULD be normalized to lowercase.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_MSSQL_INSTANCE_NAME
documentation:
The Microsoft SQL Server [instance name](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/connect/jdbc/building-the-connection-url?view=sql-server-ver15) connecting to. This name is used to determine the port of a named instance.
Note: If setting a `db.mssql.instance_name`, `server.port` is no longer required (but still recommended if non-standard).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_CASSANDRA_PAGE_SIZE
documentation: The fetch size used for paging, i.e. how many rows will be returned at once.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_CASSANDRA_CONSISTENCY_LEVEL
documentation: The consistency level of the query. Based on consistency values from [CQL](https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/dml/dmlConfigConsistency.html).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_CASSANDRA_TABLE
documentation:
The name of the primary table that the operation is acting upon, including the keyspace name (if applicable).
Note: This mirrors the db.sql.table attribute but references cassandra rather than sql. It is not recommended to attempt any client-side parsing of `db.statement` just to get this property, but it should be set if it is provided by the library being instrumented. If the operation is acting upon an anonymous table, or more than one table, this value MUST NOT be set.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_CASSANDRA_IDEMPOTENCE
documentation: Whether or not the query is idempotent.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_CASSANDRA_SPECULATIVE_EXECUTION_COUNT
documentation: The number of times a query was speculatively executed. Not set or `0` if the query was not executed speculatively.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_CASSANDRA_COORDINATOR_ID
documentation: The ID of the coordinating node for a query.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_CASSANDRA_COORDINATOR_DC
documentation: The data center of the coordinating node for a query.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_REDIS_DATABASE_INDEX
documentation: The index of the database being accessed as used in the [`SELECT` command](https://redis.io/commands/select), provided as an integer. To be used instead of the generic `db.name` attribute.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_MONGODB_COLLECTION
documentation: The collection being accessed within the database stated in `db.name`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.URL_FULL
documentation:
Absolute URL describing a network resource according to [RFC3986](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986).
Note: For network calls, URL usually has `scheme://host[:port][path][?query][#fragment]` format, where the fragment is not transmitted over HTTP, but if it is known, it should be included nevertheless.
`url.full` MUST NOT contain credentials passed via URL in form of `https://username:password@www.example.com/`. In such case username and password should be redacted and attribute's value should be `https://REDACTED:REDACTED@www.example.com/`.
`url.full` SHOULD capture the absolute URL when it is available (or can be reconstructed) and SHOULD NOT be validated or modified except for sanitizing purposes.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_SQL_TABLE
documentation:
The name of the primary table that the operation is acting upon, including the database name (if applicable).
Note: It is not recommended to attempt any client-side parsing of `db.statement` just to get this property, but it should be set if it is provided by the library being instrumented. If the operation is acting upon an anonymous table, or more than one table, this value MUST NOT be set.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_COSMOSDB_CLIENT_ID
documentation: Unique Cosmos client instance id.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_COSMOSDB_OPERATION_TYPE
documentation: CosmosDB Operation Type.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.USER_AGENT_ORIGINAL
documentation:
Full user-agent string is generated by Cosmos DB SDK. Note: The user-agent value is generated by SDK which is a combination of
`sdk_version` : Current version of SDK. e.g. 'cosmos-netstandard-sdk/3.23.0'
`direct_pkg_version` : Direct package version used by Cosmos DB SDK. e.g. '3.23.1'
`number_of_client_instances` : Number of cosmos client instances created by the application. e.g. '1'
`type_of_machine_architecture` : Machine architecture. e.g. 'X64'
`operating_system` : Operating System. e.g. 'Linux 5.4.0-1098-azure 104 18'
`runtime_framework` : Runtime Framework. e.g. '.NET Core 3.1.32'
`failover_information` : Generated key to determine if region failover enabled. Format Reg-{D (Disabled discovery)}-S(application region)|L(List of preferred regions)|N(None, user did not configure it). Default value is "NS".

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_COSMOSDB_CONNECTION_MODE
documentation: Cosmos client connection mode.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_COSMOSDB_CONTAINER
documentation: Cosmos DB container name.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_COSMOSDB_REQUEST_CONTENT_LENGTH
documentation: Request payload size in bytes.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_COSMOSDB_STATUS_CODE
documentation: Cosmos DB status code.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_COSMOSDB_SUB_STATUS_CODE
documentation: Cosmos DB sub status code.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_COSMOSDB_REQUEST_CHARGE
documentation: RU consumed for that operation.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.OTEL_STATUS_CODE
documentation: Name of the code, either "OK" or "ERROR". MUST NOT be set if the status code is UNSET.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.OTEL_STATUS_DESCRIPTION
documentation: Description of the Status if it has a value, otherwise not set.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_TRIGGER
documentation:
Type of the trigger which caused this function invocation.
Note: For the server/consumer span on the incoming side,
`faas.trigger` MUST be set.

Clients invoking FaaS instances usually cannot set `faas.trigger`,
since they would typically need to look in the payload to determine
the event type. If clients set it, it should be the same as the
trigger that corresponding incoming would have (i.e., this has
nothing to do with the underlying transport used to make the API
call to invoke the lambda, which is often HTTP).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_INVOCATION_ID
documentation: The invocation ID of the current function invocation.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CLOUD_RESOURCE_ID
documentation:
Cloud provider-specific native identifier of the monitored cloud resource (e.g. an [ARN](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-arns-and-namespaces.html) on AWS, a [fully qualified resource ID](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/resources/resources/get-by-id) on Azure, a [full resource name](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/resource_names#full_resource_name) on GCP).
Note: On some cloud providers, it may not be possible to determine the full ID at startup,
so it may be necessary to set `cloud.resource_id` as a span attribute instead.

The exact value to use for `cloud.resource_id` depends on the cloud provider.
The following well-known definitions MUST be used if you set this attribute and they apply:

* **AWS Lambda:** The function [ARN](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-arns-and-namespaces.html).
  Take care not to use the "invoked ARN" directly but replace any
  [alias suffix](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-aliases.html)
  with the resolved function version, as the same runtime instance may be invokable with
  multiple different aliases.
* **GCP:** The [URI of the resource](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/full-resource-names)
* **Azure:** The [Fully Qualified Resource ID](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/resources/resources/get-by-id) of the invoked function,
  *not* the function app, having the form
  `/subscriptions/<SUBSCIPTION_GUID>/resourceGroups/<RG>/providers/Microsoft.Web/sites/<FUNCAPP>/functions/<FUNC>`.
  This means that a span attribute MUST be used, as an Azure function app can host multiple functions that would usually share
  a TracerProvider.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_DOCUMENT_COLLECTION
documentation: The name of the source on which the triggering operation was performed. For example, in Cloud Storage or S3 corresponds to the bucket name, and in Cosmos DB to the database name.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_DOCUMENT_OPERATION
documentation: Describes the type of the operation that was performed on the data.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_DOCUMENT_TIME
documentation: A string containing the time when the data was accessed in the [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format expressed in [UTC](https://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_DOCUMENT_NAME
documentation: The document name/table subjected to the operation. For example, in Cloud Storage or S3 is the name of the file, and in Cosmos DB the table name.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.URL_PATH
documentation:
The [URI path](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986#section-3.3) component.
Note: When missing, the value is assumed to be `/`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.URL_QUERY
documentation:
The [URI query](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986#section-3.4) component.
Note: Sensitive content provided in query string SHOULD be scrubbed when instrumentations can identify it.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_SYSTEM
documentation: A string identifying the messaging system.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_OPERATION
documentation:
A string identifying the kind of messaging operation as defined in the [Operation names](#operation-names) section above.
Note: If a custom value is used, it MUST be of low cardinality.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_BATCH_MESSAGE_COUNT
documentation:
The number of messages sent, received, or processed in the scope of the batching operation.
Note: Instrumentations SHOULD NOT set `messaging.batch.message_count` on spans that operate with a single message. When a messaging client library supports both batch and single-message API for the same operation, instrumentations SHOULD use `messaging.batch.message_count` for batching APIs and SHOULD NOT use it for single-message APIs.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_CLIENT_ID
documentation: A unique identifier for the client that consumes or produces a message.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_NAME
documentation:
The message destination name.
Note: Destination name SHOULD uniquely identify a specific queue, topic or other entity within the broker. If
the broker does not have such notion, the destination name SHOULD uniquely identify the broker.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_TEMPLATE
documentation:
Low cardinality representation of the messaging destination name.
Note: Destination names could be constructed from templates. An example would be a destination name involving a user name or product id. Although the destination name in this case is of high cardinality, the underlying template is of low cardinality and can be effectively used for grouping and aggregation.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_TEMPORARY
documentation: A boolean that is true if the message destination is temporary and might not exist anymore after messages are processed.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_ANONYMOUS
documentation: A boolean that is true if the message destination is anonymous (could be unnamed or have auto-generated name).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_MESSAGE_ID
documentation: A value used by the messaging system as an identifier for the message, represented as a string.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_MESSAGE_CONVERSATION_ID
documentation: The [conversation ID](#conversations) identifying the conversation to which the message belongs, represented as a string. Sometimes called "Correlation ID".

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_MESSAGE_PAYLOAD_SIZE_BYTES
documentation: The (uncompressed) size of the message payload in bytes. Also use this attribute if it is unknown whether the compressed or uncompressed payload size is reported.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_MESSAGE_PAYLOAD_COMPRESSED_SIZE_BYTES
documentation: The compressed size of the message payload in bytes.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_TIME
documentation: A string containing the function invocation time in the [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format expressed in [UTC](https://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_CRON
documentation: A string containing the schedule period as [Cron Expression](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E12058_01/doc/doc.1014/e12030/cron_expressions.htm).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_COLDSTART
documentation: A boolean that is true if the serverless function is executed for the first time (aka cold-start).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_INVOKED_NAME
documentation:
The name of the invoked function.
Note: SHOULD be equal to the `faas.name` resource attribute of the invoked function.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_INVOKED_PROVIDER
documentation:
The cloud provider of the invoked function.
Note: SHOULD be equal to the `cloud.provider` resource attribute of the invoked function.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_INVOKED_REGION
documentation:
The cloud region of the invoked function.
Note: SHOULD be equal to the `cloud.region` resource attribute of the invoked function.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NETWORK_CONNECTION_TYPE
documentation: The internet connection type.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NETWORK_CONNECTION_SUBTYPE
documentation: This describes more details regarding the connection.type. It may be the type of cell technology connection, but it could be used for describing details about a wifi connection.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NETWORK_CARRIER_NAME
documentation: The name of the mobile carrier.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NETWORK_CARRIER_MCC
documentation: The mobile carrier country code.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NETWORK_CARRIER_MNC
documentation: The mobile carrier network code.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NETWORK_CARRIER_ICC
documentation: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 2-character country code associated with the mobile carrier network.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.PEER_SERVICE
documentation: The [`service.name`](/docs/resource/README.md#service) of the remote service. SHOULD be equal to the actual `service.name` resource attribute of the remote service if any.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.ENDUSER_ID
documentation: Username or client_id extracted from the access token or [Authorization](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-4.2) header in the inbound request from outside the system.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.ENDUSER_ROLE
documentation: Actual/assumed role the client is making the request under extracted from token or application security context.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.ENDUSER_SCOPE
documentation: Scopes or granted authorities the client currently possesses extracted from token or application security context. The value would come from the scope associated with an [OAuth 2.0 Access Token](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-3.3) or an attribute value in a [SAML 2.0 Assertion](http://docs.oasis-open.org/security/saml/Post2.0/sstc-saml-tech-overview-2.0.html).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.THREAD_ID
documentation: Current "managed" thread ID (as opposed to OS thread ID).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.THREAD_NAME
documentation: Current thread name.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CODE_FUNCTION
documentation: The method or function name, or equivalent (usually rightmost part of the code unit's name).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CODE_NAMESPACE
documentation: The "namespace" within which `code.function` is defined. Usually the qualified class or module name, such that `code.namespace` + some separator + `code.function` form a unique identifier for the code unit.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CODE_FILEPATH
documentation: The source code file name that identifies the code unit as uniquely as possible (preferably an absolute file path).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CODE_LINENO
documentation: The line number in `code.filepath` best representing the operation. It SHOULD point within the code unit named in `code.function`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CODE_COLUMN
documentation: The column number in `code.filepath` best representing the operation. It SHOULD point within the code unit named in `code.function`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_REQUEST_METHOD_ORIGINAL
documentation: Original HTTP method sent by the client in the request line.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_REQUEST_BODY_SIZE
documentation: The size of the request payload body in bytes. This is the number of bytes transferred excluding headers and is often, but not always, present as the [Content-Length](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#field.content-length) header. For requests using transport encoding, this should be the compressed size.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_RESPONSE_BODY_SIZE
documentation: The size of the response payload body in bytes. This is the number of bytes transferred excluding headers and is often, but not always, present as the [Content-Length](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#field.content-length) header. For requests using transport encoding, this should be the compressed size.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_RESEND_COUNT
documentation:
The ordinal number of request resending attempt (for any reason, including redirects).
Note: The resend count SHOULD be updated each time an HTTP request gets resent by the client, regardless of what was the cause of the resending (e.g. redirection, authorization failure, 503 Server Unavailable, network issues, or any other).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_SYSTEM
documentation: The value `aws-api`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_SERVICE
documentation:
The name of the service to which a request is made, as returned by the AWS SDK.
Note: This is the logical name of the service from the RPC interface perspective, which can be different from the name of any implementing class. The `code.namespace` attribute may be used to store the latter (despite the attribute name, it may include a class name; e.g., class with method actually executing the call on the server side, RPC client stub class on the client side).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_METHOD
documentation:
The name of the operation corresponding to the request, as returned by the AWS SDK.
Note: This is the logical name of the method from the RPC interface perspective, which can be different from the name of any implementing method/function. The `code.function` attribute may be used to store the latter (e.g., method actually executing the call on the server side, RPC client stub method on the client side).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_REQUEST_ID
documentation: The AWS request ID as returned in the response headers `x-amz-request-id` or `x-amz-requestid`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_TABLE_NAMES
documentation: The keys in the `RequestItems` object field.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_CONSUMED_CAPACITY
documentation: The JSON-serialized value of each item in the `ConsumedCapacity` response field.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_ITEM_COLLECTION_METRICS
documentation: The JSON-serialized value of the `ItemCollectionMetrics` response field.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_PROVISIONED_READ_CAPACITY
documentation: The value of the `ProvisionedThroughput.ReadCapacityUnits` request parameter.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_PROVISIONED_WRITE_CAPACITY
documentation: The value of the `ProvisionedThroughput.WriteCapacityUnits` request parameter.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_CONSISTENT_READ
documentation: The value of the `ConsistentRead` request parameter.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_PROJECTION
documentation: The value of the `ProjectionExpression` request parameter.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_LIMIT
documentation: The value of the `Limit` request parameter.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_ATTRIBUTES_TO_GET
documentation: The value of the `AttributesToGet` request parameter.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_INDEX_NAME
documentation: The value of the `IndexName` request parameter.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_SELECT
documentation: The value of the `Select` request parameter.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_GLOBAL_SECONDARY_INDEXES
documentation: The JSON-serialized value of each item of the `GlobalSecondaryIndexes` request field.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_LOCAL_SECONDARY_INDEXES
documentation: The JSON-serialized value of each item of the `LocalSecondaryIndexes` request field.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_EXCLUSIVE_START_TABLE
documentation: The value of the `ExclusiveStartTableName` request parameter.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_TABLE_COUNT
documentation: The the number of items in the `TableNames` response parameter.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_SCAN_FORWARD
documentation: The value of the `ScanIndexForward` request parameter.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_SEGMENT
documentation: The value of the `Segment` request parameter.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_TOTAL_SEGMENTS
documentation: The value of the `TotalSegments` request parameter.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_COUNT
documentation: The value of the `Count` response parameter.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_SCANNED_COUNT
documentation: The value of the `ScannedCount` response parameter.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_ATTRIBUTE_DEFINITIONS
documentation: The JSON-serialized value of each item in the `AttributeDefinitions` request field.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_GLOBAL_SECONDARY_INDEX_UPDATES
documentation: The JSON-serialized value of each item in the the `GlobalSecondaryIndexUpdates` request field.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_S3_BUCKET
documentation:
The S3 bucket name the request refers to. Corresponds to the `--bucket` parameter of the [S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/index.html) operations.
Note: The `bucket` attribute is applicable to all S3 operations that reference a bucket, i.e. that require the bucket name as a mandatory parameter.
This applies to almost all S3 operations except `list-buckets`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_S3_KEY
documentation:
The S3 object key the request refers to. Corresponds to the `--key` parameter of the [S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/index.html) operations.
Note: The `key` attribute is applicable to all object-related S3 operations, i.e. that require the object key as a mandatory parameter.
This applies in particular to the following operations:

- [copy-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/copy-object.html)
- [delete-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/delete-object.html)
- [get-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/get-object.html)
- [head-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/head-object.html)
- [put-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/put-object.html)
- [restore-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/restore-object.html)
- [select-object-content](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/select-object-content.html)
- [abort-multipart-upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/abort-multipart-upload.html)
- [complete-multipart-upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/complete-multipart-upload.html)
- [create-multipart-upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/create-multipart-upload.html)
- [list-parts](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/list-parts.html)
- [upload-part](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part.html)
- [upload-part-copy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part-copy.html).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_S3_COPY_SOURCE
documentation:
The source object (in the form `bucket`/`key`) for the copy operation.
Note: The `copy_source` attribute applies to S3 copy operations and corresponds to the `--copy-source` parameter
of the [copy-object operation within the S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/copy-object.html).
This applies in particular to the following operations:

- [copy-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/copy-object.html)
- [upload-part-copy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part-copy.html).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_S3_UPLOAD_ID
documentation:
Upload ID that identifies the multipart upload.
Note: The `upload_id` attribute applies to S3 multipart-upload operations and corresponds to the `--upload-id` parameter
of the [S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/index.html) multipart operations.
This applies in particular to the following operations:

- [abort-multipart-upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/abort-multipart-upload.html)
- [complete-multipart-upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/complete-multipart-upload.html)
- [list-parts](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/list-parts.html)
- [upload-part](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part.html)
- [upload-part-copy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part-copy.html).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_S3_DELETE
documentation:
The delete request container that specifies the objects to be deleted.
Note: The `delete` attribute is only applicable to the [delete-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/delete-object.html) operation.
The `delete` attribute corresponds to the `--delete` parameter of the
[delete-objects operation within the S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/delete-objects.html).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_S3_PART_NUMBER
documentation:
The part number of the part being uploaded in a multipart-upload operation. This is a positive integer between 1 and 10,000.
Note: The `part_number` attribute is only applicable to the [upload-part](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part.html)
and [upload-part-copy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part-copy.html) operations.
The `part_number` attribute corresponds to the `--part-number` parameter of the
[upload-part operation within the S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part.html).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.GRAPHQL_OPERATION_NAME
documentation: The name of the operation being executed.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.GRAPHQL_OPERATION_TYPE
documentation: The type of the operation being executed.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.GRAPHQL_DOCUMENT
documentation:
The GraphQL document being executed.
Note: The value may be sanitized to exclude sensitive information.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_RABBITMQ_DESTINATION_ROUTING_KEY
documentation: RabbitMQ message routing key.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_MESSAGE_KEY
documentation:
Message keys in Kafka are used for grouping alike messages to ensure they're processed on the same partition. They differ from `messaging.message.id` in that they're not unique. If the key is `null`, the attribute MUST NOT be set.
Note: If the key type is not string, it's string representation has to be supplied for the attribute. If the key has no unambiguous, canonical string form, don't include its value.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_CONSUMER_GROUP
documentation: Name of the Kafka Consumer Group that is handling the message. Only applies to consumers, not producers.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_DESTINATION_PARTITION
documentation: Partition the message is sent to.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_MESSAGE_OFFSET
documentation: The offset of a record in the corresponding Kafka partition.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_MESSAGE_TOMBSTONE
documentation: A boolean that is true if the message is a tombstone.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_NAMESPACE
documentation: Namespace of RocketMQ resources, resources in different namespaces are individual.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_CLIENT_GROUP
documentation: Name of the RocketMQ producer/consumer group that is handling the message. The client type is identified by the SpanKind.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_DELIVERY_TIMESTAMP
documentation: The timestamp in milliseconds that the delay message is expected to be delivered to consumer.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_DELAY_TIME_LEVEL
documentation: The delay time level for delay message, which determines the message delay time.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_GROUP
documentation: It is essential for FIFO message. Messages that belong to the same message group are always processed one by one within the same consumer group.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_TYPE
documentation: Type of message.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_TAG
documentation: The secondary classifier of message besides topic.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_KEYS
documentation: Key(s) of message, another way to mark message besides message id.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_CONSUMPTION_MODEL
documentation: Model of message consumption. This only applies to consumer spans.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_GRPC_STATUS_CODE
documentation: The [numeric status code](https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/v1.33.2/doc/statuscodes.md) of the gRPC request.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_JSONRPC_VERSION
documentation: Protocol version as in `jsonrpc` property of request/response. Since JSON-RPC 1.0 does not specify this, the value can be omitted.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_JSONRPC_REQUEST_ID
documentation: `id` property of request or response. Since protocol allows id to be int, string, `null` or missing (for notifications), value is expected to be cast to string for simplicity. Use empty string in case of `null` value. Omit entirely if this is a notification.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_JSONRPC_ERROR_CODE
documentation: `error.code` property of response if it is an error response.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_JSONRPC_ERROR_MESSAGE
documentation: `error.message` property of response if it is an error response.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGE_TYPE
documentation: Whether this is a received or sent message.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGE_ID
documentation:
MUST be calculated as two different counters starting from `1` one for sent messages and one for received message.
Note: This way we guarantee that the values will be consistent between different implementations.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGE_COMPRESSED_SIZE
documentation: Compressed size of the message in bytes.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGE_UNCOMPRESSED_SIZE
documentation: Uncompressed size of the message in bytes.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_CONNECT_RPC_ERROR_CODE
documentation: The [error codes](https://connect.build/docs/protocol/#error-codes) of the Connect request. Error codes are always string values.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.EXCEPTION_ESCAPED
documentation:
SHOULD be set to true if the exception event is recorded at a point where it is known that the exception is escaping the scope of the span.
Note: An exception is considered to have escaped (or left) the scope of a span,
if that span is ended while the exception is still logically "in flight".
This may be actually "in flight" in some languages (e.g. if the exception
is passed to a Context manager's `__exit__` method in Python) but will
usually be caught at the point of recording the exception in most languages.

It is usually not possible to determine at the point where an exception is thrown
whether it will escape the scope of a span.
However, it is trivial to know that an exception
will escape, if one checks for an active exception just before ending the span,
as done in the [example above](#recording-an-exception).

It follows that an exception may still escape the scope of the span
even if the `exception.escaped` attribute was not set or set to false,
since the event might have been recorded at a time where it was not
clear whether the exception will escape.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.URL_FRAGMENT
documentation: The [URI fragment](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986#section-3.5) component.
